category_title: Elementary students with anger management skill deficits and bullying behaviors
minimal_context: Amy is participating in a group activity with peers during the after-school program.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Amy, please join the other students for the after-school program activity.

STUDENT: They aren't doing what I say. I feel out of control.

TUTOR: Just keep working with your group.

STUDENT: I'm getting angry! I'm going to end up 'getting in trouble' again.

TUTOR: Please try to get along with them.

STUDENT: (Verbally threatens a peer and physically hits another student) Leave me alone!

options.A: Provide the student with her favorite color crayon, purple, to use during the after-school program activity with her peers.
options.B: Send the student to a quiet place to fill out a 'Cool Down Worksheet' describing the infraction, why she did it, and what to do differently. Review this with her during the next unstructured period.
options.C: Bring in a mirror to help the student see the reflection of her angry expressions and learn to interact in the right way with her peers during the after-school program.
options.D: Ask a direct question about the specific component of the peer interaction that was ignored ('Why hit them, then?') during the after-school program activity.
